discrepancies.summary: Relay 066 prompt body, LANE-2 R1 wording (lane2_meta_verdict.md L42), and 064 supplement (phase_a_supplementary_deg_a_zero.md section 5 L222) all reference 'PCF-1 v1.3 §6 Theorem 5'. In the canonical 16pp source p12_pcf1_main.tex (SHA E83BB377F297DBF0..), the WKB theorem and its row table reside at \section{The WKB Exponent Identity}\label{sec:wkb} (L516), which is the FIFTH section of the .tex file (counting \section{...} ordering: §1 Introduction L100, §2 Spec(K) L190, §3 Sharp Dichotomy L272, §4 Structural Evidence L359, §5 WKB Exponent Identity L516, §6 V_quad as the Explicit Prototype L611, §7 AEAL Evidence Chain L720, §8 Open Questions L776). The 'Sharp Dichotomy and Conjecture A v5' content named in the relay 066 prompt body actually resides in §3 (sec:dichotomy). The thm:wkb numbering under the amsart preamble \newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section] / \newtheorem{conjecture}[theorem]{Conjecture} renders as 'Theorem 5.1' in the §5 namespace; project shorthand 'Theorem 5' resolves to the same SHA-anchored statement.
discrepancies.resolution: Pcf1_v13_v_quad_row_reframing.md §2 quotes the canonical 16pp .tex by SHA + line range (L516, L528-548, L566-577, L578-583), which resolves the shorthand precisely. Project-shorthand wording '§6 Theorem 5' is preserved in surface prose for cross-anchor consistency with R1 + 064 + relay prompt body.

discrepancies.summary: Relay 066 P5 substrate (b) names p12_journal_main.tex L575-957 (workspace v1.4 working draft, 72311 B, SHA 82173A09521D6676..) as a possible row-table fallback. Inspection at fire time finds the v1.4 working draft does NOT contain the WKB row table tab:wkb-exponents at the cited L575-957 region; the v1.4 draft has been reorganised (V_quad mentions appear at L86, L231, L292, L625, L775, L810, L819, L850, L921, L937, L939, L958, L962, L993, L998, L1006, L1011, L1031, L1077, L1108; the L625/L775 tabular rows are spec-table content, not the WKB exponents row table). Substrate path (a) was used (canonical 16pp v1.3 .tex at SHA E83BB377F297DBF0..), per relay 066 P5's 'fall back to (a) only' rule.
discrepancies.resolution: P5 substrate path (a) selected; row table quoted verbatim from canonical 16pp v1.3 .tex L566-577 with full SHA anchor. G12 PCF1-V13-RECONCILE jurisdiction is unchanged.

discrepancies.category: section-index numbering ambiguity within project corpus
discrepancies.summary: The project has multiple PCF-1 source paths with divergent §-index numbering: (i) canonical 16pp v1.3 source at sessions/2026-05-01/PCF1-V13-UPDATE/p12_pcf1_main.tex (SHA E83BB377..) has 8 sections, with WKB at §5; (ii) v1.4 working draft at tex/submitted/p12_journal_main.tex (SHA 82173A09..) has different section structure with V_quad-specific section at §6 ('The V_quad Painlevé Prototype' near L958). The R1 wording '§6 Theorem 5' may have inherited the §-index from a v1.4-or-later draft layout where V_quad-prototype section is §6 and WKB content was previously included; not directly verifiable without source-archaeology on intermediate v1.x drafts.
discrepancies.resolution: 066 §2 anchors entirely by SHA + L-numbers in the canonical 16pp v1.3 source; no §-index drift can affect the substrate citation. D3 surfaced for operator awareness; G12 PCF1-V13-RECONCILE may resolve §-index canonical wording at v1.4 dispatch time.

discrepancies.summary: Relay 066 P3 cites 064 deposit at bridge 6a150b6. Bridge log shows 6a150b6 shipped both 064 (PHASE-A-DEG_A-ZERO-SUPPLEMENTARY) and 065 (PCF2-CF_VALUE-AUDIT-9IMPLS) under the same hash; addendum c1b8c54 followed (PCF2-CF_VALUE-AUDIT-9IMPLS-065 -- handoff.md addendum: A0 anomaly noting commit 6a150b6 shipped 065 + parallel 064 in same hash). 066 reads from individual SHA-anchored files within sessions/2026-05-06/PHASE-A-DEG_A-ZERO-SUPPLEMENTARY-064/ which are bit-identical to the 6a150b6-time content; commit-hash entanglement does not affect file-level SHAs.
discrepancies.resolution: 066 P3 verification reads file SHAs directly (80E28568FF142B1A.. for the 064 supplement), not commit-level hashes; D4 is meta-recordkeeping rather than substrate drift. No re-fire required.
